mission.title: valkey: Support field level expire/TTL for hash, set and sorted set
mission.description: Support field level expire/TTL for hash, set and sorted set. Requested by 115+ users.

steps.title: Check current valkey setup
steps.description:
Verify your valkey version and configuration:
```bash
valkey-server --version
```
This feature requires a working valkey installation.

steps.title: Review valkey configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant valkey configuration:
**The problem/use-case that the feature addresses**
My customer has a use case where each element within a Hash/Set/Sorted Set data type would have different TTL. Right now, it is done by manually write client side logic or lua script.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support field level expire/TTL for hash, set and sorted set
steps.description:
PTAL

This is a first draft of the robj hack.

There's a lot of duplicated code with createEmbeddedStringObject, but it's a PoC.

The EMBSTR is compact, but other robj are created with an expire field set to -1 (meaning no expire) and an embedded key pointer set to NULL.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Support field level expire/TTL for hash, set and sorted set" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
PTAL

This is a first draft of the robj hack.

There's a lot of duplicated code with createEmbeddedStringObject, but it's a PoC.

The EMBSTR is compact, but other robj are created with an expire field set to -1 (meaning no expire) and an embedded key pointer set to NULL.

prerequisites.description: A working valkey installation or development environment.
